title: And, bhikkhus, what is right view? Bhikkhus, knowledge of suffering, knowledge of the origin of suffering, knowledge of the cessation of suffering, knowledge of the…
content: And, bhikkhus, what is right view? Bhikkhus, knowledge of suffering, knowledge of the origin of suffering, knowledge of the cessation of suffering, knowledge of the path leading to the cessation of suffering. Bhikkhus, this is called right view. And, bhikkhus, what is right intention? Bhikkhus, intention of renunciation, intention of non-ill-will, intention of harmlessness. Bhikkhus, this is called right intention. And, bhikkhus, what is right speech? Bhikkhus, abstaining from false speech, abstaining from divisive speech, abstaining from harsh speech, abstaining from idle chatter. Bhikkhus, this is called right speech. And, bhikkhus, what is right action? Bhikkhus, abstaining from killing living beings, abstaining from taking what is not given, abstaining from sexual misconduct. Bhikkhus, this is called right action. And, bhikkhus, what is right livelihood? Here, bhikkhus, a noble disciple, having abandoned wrong livelihood, earns his living by right livelihood. Bhikkhus, this is called right livelihood.

title: And, Puṇṇa, what is neither-dark-nor-bright action with a neither-dark-nor-bright result, action leading to the cessation of action? Here, Puṇṇa, the volition (cetanā) that abandons dark…
content: And, Puṇṇa, what is neither-dark-nor-bright action with a neither-dark-nor-bright result, action leading to the cessation of action? Here, Puṇṇa, the volition (cetanā) that abandons dark action with its dark result, the volition that abandons bright action with its bright result, the volition that abandons dark-and-bright action with its dark-and-bright result — this, Puṇṇa, is called neither-dark-nor-bright action with a neither-dark-nor-bright result, action leading to the cessation of action. Puṇṇa, these four kinds of action have been directly known, realized, and proclaimed by me.

title: Bhikkhus, the eye is impermanent, changing, altering. The ear is impermanent, changing, altering. The nose is impermanent, changing, altering. The tongue is impermanent, changing, altering.…
content: Bhikkhus, the eye is impermanent, changing, altering. The ear is impermanent, changing, altering. The nose is impermanent, changing, altering. The tongue is impermanent, changing, altering. The body is impermanent, changing, altering. The mind is impermanent, changing, altering. Bhikkhus, one who has faith and conviction in these things is called a faith-follower, who has entered the fixed course of rightness, entered the plane of true persons, transcended the plane of ordinary persons. He cannot do any action that would cause him to be reborn in hell, the animal realm, or the ghost realm; he cannot pass away without realizing the fruit of stream-entry. One who, with wisdom, has a degree of acceptance of these things is called a Dhamma-follower, who has entered the fixed course of rightness, entered the plane of true persons, transcended the plane of ordinary persons. He cannot do any action that would cause him to be reborn in hell, the animal realm, or the ghost realm; he cannot pass away without realizing the fruit of stream-entry. One who knows and sees these things as they are is called a stream-enterer, no longer subject to falling, bound for enlightenment.

title: 'Friend, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and they do not experience one another's domain and range. That is, the eye, ear, nose,…
content: 'Friend, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and they do not experience one another's domain and range. That is, the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body. Friend, since these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and do not experience one another's domain and range, what is their support, and what experiences their domain and range?' — 'Friend, these five faculties have different domains, different ranges, and they do not experience one another's domain and range, namely, the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body. Friend, among these five faculties, the mind is their support, and the mind experiences their domain and range.'

title: Elder Cūḷaka: Peacocks call, beautiful-crested, with lovely tail feathers, Their necks bright blue, beaks beautiful, voices melodious. This land is well covered with grass, well…
content:
Elder Cūḷaka:

Peacocks call, beautiful-crested, with lovely tail feathers,
Their necks bright blue, beaks beautiful, voices melodious.
This land is well covered with grass, well moistened with water,
Well covered by the sky, well covered by rain clouds.

The human body is well healthy, the mind good, practicing jhāna.
Excellent is the well-arisen mind in the Buddha's well-spoken teaching.
Experience the path leading to the Deathless,
That path is unsurpassed, the pure, healthy,
Subtle, fine path, well seen.

title: - Poṭṭhapāda, there are three kinds of self-identification: the gross self-identification, the mind-made self-identification, and the formless self-identification. Poṭṭhapāda, what is the gross self-identification? Having…
content: - Poṭṭhapāda, there are three kinds of self-identification: the gross self-identification, the mind-made self-identification, and the formless self-identification. Poṭṭhapāda, what is the gross self-identification? Having form, composed of the four great elements, nourished by solid food — this is the gross self-identification. What is the mind-made self-identification? Having form, mind-made, complete with all major and minor parts, with all faculties intact — this is the mind-made self-identification. What is the formless self-identification? Formless, made of perception — this is the formless self-identification.

title: Thus, Ānanda, action is the field, consciousness is the seed, craving is the moisture. For beings obstructed by ignorance and fettered by craving, consciousness is…
content: Thus, Ānanda, action is the field, consciousness is the seed, craving is the moisture. For beings obstructed by ignorance and fettered by craving, consciousness is established in a lower realm. Thus there is the production of renewed existence in the future. Thus, Ānanda, existence comes to be.

title: A bhikkhu, bhikkhus, who has reverence, deference, and lives in concord with his fellow celibates — it is possible that he will fulfill the practice…
content: A bhikkhu, bhikkhus, who has reverence, deference, and lives in concord with his fellow celibates — it is possible that he will fulfill the practice of superior conduct. Having fulfilled the practice of superior conduct, it is possible that he will fulfill the learner's practice. Having fulfilled the learner's practice, it is possible that he will fulfill the rules of virtuous conduct. Having fulfilled the rules of virtuous conduct, it is possible that he will fulfill right view. Having fulfilled right view, it is possible that he will fulfill right concentration.

title: Elder Kātiyāna: O Kātiyāna, wake up, sit up! Do not sleep too much; awaken yourself! O heedless relatives, the passive, the lazy — Do not…
content:
Elder Kātiyāna:

O Kātiyāna, wake up, sit up!
Do not sleep too much; awaken yourself!
O heedless relatives, the passive, the lazy —
Do not let Death deceive and conquer you.

Like waves covering the great ocean,
So birth and old age cover and bury you.
Make yourself a safe island,
For no one else can serve and help you as a refuge.

The Teacher has laid down the path crossing beyond bonds,
Crossing beyond the fear of birth and old age.
In the evening and in the morning, live heedfully,
Attentive, persisting, in devoted effort.

Abandoning former bonds, wearing the outer robe,
Head shaven smooth, eating almsfood,
Do not delight in playing, do not indulge in sleeping.
Strive in meditation, O Kātiyāna!

Meditate, be victorious, O Kātiyāna!
Be skilled in the path, secure from bonds.
Attain supreme purity, you will attain Nibbāna,
Like water extinguishing fire.

Light is produced, the radiance is still weak,
Like a reed bent down by the wind.
O relative of Suda, do not grasp thus!
Expel Māra, dispassionate toward all feelings,
Wait for your time! Here you will be cooled.

title: Furthermore, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of…
content: Furthermore, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of suffering. For, bhikkhus, the training rules for the holy life have been laid down by me for the disciples, for the complete and proper ending of suffering. Therefore, a disciple, having undertaken and trained in the training rules, should have unimpaired conduct, uncut conduct, unstained conduct, unblemished conduct in that training. Thus, bhikkhus, is the benefit of training.
